original_text: Several key risks temper our outlook. Revenue growth has shown notable volatility across 2025 quarters, ranging from -0.93% in Q1 to 7.22% in Q2 before moderating to 0.91% in Q3, suggesting top-line performance may remain inconsistent. Additionally, regulatory developments in the competitive payments landscape present uncertainty, particularly the Visa-Mastercard proposed settlement over interchange fees announced on November 12, 2025, which could potentially impact rewards credit card dynamics across the industry. The company operates in the highly regulated financial services sector, entailing ongoing compliance and operational execution risks.
revised_text: Several key risks temper our outlook. Revenue growth has shown notable volatility across 2025 quarters, ranging from -0.93% in Q1 to 7.22% in Q2 before moderating to 0.91% in Q3, suggesting top-line performance may remain inconsistent. Additionally, regulatory developments in the competitive payments landscape present uncertainty, particularly the Visa-Mastercard proposed settlement over interchange fees announced on November 12, 2025, which could potentially impact rewards credit card dynamics across the industry. The company operates in the highly regulated financial services sector, entailing ongoing compliance and operational execution risks.
changes_summary: Removed C031 from cited chunk references as it contains information about American Express Small Business Saturday grants program that does not support any claims in the paragraph. Retained C030 which supports the Visa-Mastercard settlement claim and K001 which supports the revenue growth volatility calculations. Paragraph text remains unchanged as all factual claims are properly supported by the remaining evidence.
